source_pr.title: memory: fix data race & enhance behaviors in arbitrator
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: ref #58194 Problem Summary: - The statement tracker is cached and reset after each SQL execution. The session related root pool of the mem-arbitrator should NOT ref such tracker. ### What changed and how does it work? - Remove `trackerArbitrateHelper` to decouple `*Tracker` and `ArbitrationContext` - Add a more efficient method `SampleRuntimeMemStats` to read runtime mem stats without STW (compare with `ForceReadMemStats`) - Enhance the mem arbitration by cleaning plan cache in time. ### Test ### tpcc - Env - pd - cpu: 4000m memory: 8Gi - tidb - cpu: 8000m memory: 16Gi - tikv x 3 - cpu: 8000m memory: 32Gi ``` workloadType: type : [ 'tpcc' ] db : tpcc warehouse : 1000 threads : 3000 ignoreErrors : 'all' runTime: totalRunTime: 1h ``` ### tpcc tidb x 2 - Env - pd - cpu: 4000m memory: 8Gi - tidb x 2 - cpu: 8000m memory: 16Gi - tikv x 3 - cpu: 8000m memory: 32Gi ``` workloadType: type : [ 'tpcc' ] db : tpcc warehouse : 1000 threads : 6000 ignoreErrors : 'all' runTime: totalRunTime: 1h ``` ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No need to test > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed. > Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```
